title: Trichera dumetorum
taxonomy: Trichera dumetorum is classified within the family Orobanchaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes. This family is a subfamily within the order Lamiales, which comprises a diverse range of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further classified within the clade Asterids, a large and widespread clade of flowering plants. The clade Asterids is part of the larger group of angiosperms, the flowering plants. This classification is based on the shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships among these plant species.

Its bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat different ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
